title: MD
biography: Carlos Finlay was a Cuban physician who first proposed that mosquitoes were the vector transmitting yellow fever, a theory later confirmed by Walter Reed's experiments. His discovery was foundational to the control of mosquito-borne diseases and enabled the construction of the Panama Canal.
aiSummary: Carlos Finlay was an epidemiology specialist who discovered that mosquitoes transmit yellow fever. His work enabled control of tropical diseases and construction of the Panama Canal. Based in Havana, Cuba.
techniquesInvented: Mosquito-vector theory of yellow fever transmission

affiliations.hospitalName: Academy of Medical, Physical and Natural Sciences of Havana

awards: UNESCO Carlos J. Finlay Prize (named in his honor)

timeline.title: Proposed mosquito-vector theory of yellow fever
timeline.description: Presented theory that Aedes aegypti mosquitoes transmit yellow fever at the International Sanitary Conference

timeline.title: Theory confirmed by Walter Reed
timeline.description: US Army Yellow Fever Commission confirmed Finlay's mosquito transmission theory

timeline.title: Appointed Chief Health Officer of Cuba
timeline.description: Led successful yellow fever eradication campaign in Cuba
